content:
name: Deploy to GitHub Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: "pages"
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build with Eleventy
        run: npm run build

      - name: Upload Pages artifact
        uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: _site

  deploy:
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4

node_modules/
_site/
.DS_Store
*.log
.env
.env.*

# StageHaus Productions — Website > Built with [Eleventy (11ty)](https://www.11ty.dev/) · Nunjucks templating · GitHub Pages deployment --- ## Table of Contents 1. [Project Overview](#project-overview) 2. [Prerequisites](#prerequisites) 3. [Local Development](#local-development) 4. [Project Structure](#project-structure) 5. [Deploying to GitHub Pages](#deploying-to-github-pages) 6. [Adding New Pages](#adding-new-pages) 7. [Activating Phase 2 Pages](#activating-phase-2-pages) 8. [Artist Template Usage](#artist-template-usage) 9. [Merch Store Integration](#merch-store-integration) 10. [Content Updates](#content-updates) 11. [Environment & Configuration](#environment--configuration) --- ## Project Overview StageHaus Productions website — a fully static site generated by [Eleventy 2.x](https://www.11ty.dev/) using Nunjucks templating. All pages share a common base layout, header, footer, and design system. The site is optimized for GitHub Pages deployment via GitHub Actions. **Tech Stack:** - **Generator:** Eleventy 2.0.1 - **Templating:** Nunjucks (.njk) - **CSS:** Vanilla CSS with custom properties design system - **JS:** Vanilla ES6+ (no frameworks, no bundler) - **Deployment:** GitHub Pages via GitHub Actions - **Fonts:** Google Fonts (Cormorant Garamond + Inter + JetBrains Mono) - **Payments (scaffold):** Square Web Payments SDK / Stripe.js --- ## Prerequisites - **Node.js** 18 or higher — [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org/) - **npm** 9 or higher (comes with Node.js) - A **GitHub account** with a repository for the site --- ## Local Development ```bash # 1. Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/stagehaus.git cd stagehaus # 2. Install dependencies npm install # 3. Start the development server npm start # → Runs at http://localhost:8080 with live reload ``` **Available scripts:** | Command | Description | |---|---| | `npm start` | Start dev server at localhost:8080 with watch | | `npm run dev` | Alias for `npm start` | | `npm run build` | Production build → `_site/` | | `npm run clean` | Delete `_site/` directory | The dev server watches all source files and reloads the browser automatically. --- ## Project Structure ``` stagehaus-v2/ ├── .eleventy.js # Eleventy config (filters, shortcodes, collections) ├── .gitignore ├── .github/ │ └── workflows/ │ └── deploy.yml # GitHub Actions: build + deploy to Pages ├── package.json ├── README.md └── src/ # All source files ├── _data/ │ ├── site.json # Global site config (name, email, phone, stats, social) │ └── navigation.json # Nav structure, all service definitions (phase flags) ├── _includes/ │ ├── layouts/ │ │ └── base.njk # Base HTML5 layout (all pages extend this) │ ├── partials/ │ │ ├── header.njk # Fixed nav with mega-menu + mobile menu │ │ ├── footer.njk # 4-column footer with social links │ │ └── cta-band.njk # Reusable CTA section (configured via set variables) │ └── macros/ │ └── components.njk # All reusable Nunjucks macros ├── assets/ │ ├── css/ │ │ └── style.css # Full design system CSS (~40KB) │ ├── js/ │ │ └── main.js # Site JS (nav, scroll, counters, forms) │ └── images/ # Static images (place hero/logo images here) ├── artist-template/ # Standalone artist site template │ ├── index.html # "Midnight" variant template │ └── style.css # Standalone CSS for template ├── index.njk # Homepage ├── about.njk ├── artist-management.njk ├── booking-strategy.njk ├── show-logistics.njk ├── merch-operations.njk ├── website-development.njk ├── contact.njk ├── event-production.njk # Phase 2 — Coming Soon ├── artist-hospitality.njk # Phase 2 — Coming Soon ├── vendor-coordination.njk # Phase 2 — Coming Soon ├── festival-operations.njk # Phase 2 — Coming Soon └── creative-direction.njk # Phase 2 — Coming Soon ``` --- ## Deploying to GitHub Pages ### First-Time Setup 1. **Create a GitHub repository** for the site (public or private) 2. **Enable GitHub Pages** in the repo settings: - Go to `Settings → Pages` - Under **Source**, select **GitHub Actions** 3. **Push the code:** ```bash git init git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/stagehaus.git git add . git commit -m "Initial commit — StageHaus site" git push -u origin main ``` 4. The GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`) automatically: - Installs Node dependencies - Runs `npm run build` - Deploys the `_site/` output to GitHub Pages 5. **Your site will be live at:** `https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/stagehaus/` ### Custom Domain 1. Add your domain in `Settings → Pages → Custom domain` 2. Create a `CNAME` file in `src/` containing your domain: ``` stagehaus.com ``` 3. Add the file to Eleventy's passthrough in `.eleventy.js`: ```js eleventyConfig.addPassthroughCopy("src/CNAME"); ``` 4. Configure your DNS: add a CNAME record pointing to `YOUR_USERNAME.github.io` ### Pathprefix (Subdirectory Deployments) If the site is deployed at a subdirectory (e.g., `github.io/stagehaus/`), the base URL for assets must match. Check `.eleventy.js` for the `pathPrefix` setting and update if needed. --- ## Adding New Pages ### Phase 1 Page (Full Content) 1. Create `src/your-page-name.njk` 2. Use this front matter template: ```yaml --- layout: base.njk title: Page Title — StageHaus Productions pageDescription: SEO description (150–160 chars) currentPage: your-page-name bodyClass: page-your-page-name tags: phase1 --- ``` 3. Import macros at the top: ```njk {% from "macros/components.njk" import pageHero, processStep, valueCard %} ``` 4. Use `{{ pageHero("Label", "Title", "Subtitle", "Breadcrumb", "/url/") }}` for the hero 5. The page is automatically added to the `phase1` collection and will build to `/your-page-name/index.html` ### Using the CTA Band Partial Set these variables before including `cta-band.njk`: ```njk {% set ctaSectionLabel = "Label text" %} {% set ctaHeading = "Main heading" %} {% set ctaSubtext = "Supporting paragraph" %} {% set ctaPrimaryLabel = "Primary button text" %} {% set ctaSecondaryLabel = "Secondary button text" %} {% set ctaSecondaryUrl = "/target-url/" %} {% include "partials/cta-band.njk" %} ``` --- ## Activating Phase 2 Pages Phase 2 pages are already built and deployed as "Coming Soon" placeholders. To convert a Phase 2 page to a full content page: 1. **Update the page file** (e.g., `src/event-production.njk`): - Replace the `coming-soon` section with full page content - Change `tags: phase2` to `tags: phase1` 2. **Update `src/_data/navigation.json`:** - Find the service entry (e.g., `"key": "event-production"`) - Set `"comingSoon": false` - Set `"phase": 1` 3. **Rebuild and deploy:** ```bash npm run build git add . && git commit -m "Activate Event Production page" && git push ``` The navigation mega-menu automatically moves the service from "Coming Soon" to "Active Services" based on the `comingSoon` flag. --- ## Artist Template Usage The `src/artist-template/` directory contains a standalone static HTML/CSS artist site template ("Midnight" variant). It requires no build step. ### Deploying a Template for an Artist 1. **Copy the template directory** to a new folder: ```bash cp -r src/artist-template/ my-artist-site/ ``` 2. **Customize `index.html`** — replace all `[PLACEHOLDER]` values: - `[ARTIST NAME]` — artist or band name - `[ARTIST GENRE]` — genre and location string - `[ARTIST BIO]` — biography paragraphs - `[ALBUM TITLE]`, `[SINGLE TITLE]`, etc. — music catalog - Show dates in the `#shows` section - Social media links in the nav and footer 3. **Add real images:** - Create an `images/` folder inside the template directory - `images/hero-bg.jpg` — hero background (1920×1080px, compressed WebP preferred) - `images/artist-photo.jpg` — about section portrait - `images/album-art.jpg` — release artwork - Update `style.css` and `index.html` to reference these files 4. **Deploy options:** - **GitHub Pages:** Push to a repo, enable Pages from the root or `/docs` folder - **Netlify Drop:** Drag the folder to [netlify.com/drop](https://app.netlify.com/drop) - **Vercel:** `npx vercel` from the template directory ### Adding to the StageHaus Template Page To display the template as a new card on the `/website-development/` page: 1. Add a new `.artist-template-card` block in `src/website-development.njk` 2. Update the template grid in `src/assets/css/style.css` if needed --- ## Merch Store Integration The `/merch-operations/` page includes a scaffold for an artist storefront. To wire up real payments: ### Square Web Payments SDK ```html ``` ```js // In main.js or a page-specific script const payments = Square.payments('YOUR_APP_ID', 'YOUR_LOCATION_ID'); // Attach payment form to .merch-card__buy buttons ``` Full docs: [developer.squareup.com/docs/web-payments/overview](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/web-payments/overview) ### Stripe ```html ``` ```js const stripe = Stripe('YOUR_PUBLISHABLE_KEY'); // Use Stripe Payment Element or Checkout ``` Full docs: [stripe.com/docs/payments/payment-element](https://stripe.com/docs/payments/payment-element) ### Product Data via JSON To drive products from data instead of hardcoded HTML: 1. Create `src/_data/merch.json`: ```json { "products": [ { "title": "Classic Logo Tee", "price": "$35.00", "sku": "tee-001", "image": "tee.jpg", "meta": "Unisex · XS–3XL", "inStock": true }, { "title": "Tour Hoodie", "price": "$75.00", "sku": "hoodie-001", "image": "hoodie.jpg", "meta": "Heavyweight · S–2XL", "inStock": true } ] } ``` 2. Loop in `merch-operations.njk`: ```njk {% for product in merch.products %} {% if product.inStock %}

{{ product.title }}

{{ product.meta }}

{{ product.price }}
{% endif %} {% endfor %} ``` --- ## Content Updates ### Update Site Info Edit `src/_data/site.json` for global changes: - Company name, tagline, contact info, social links - Stats displayed in the hero and stat bars ### Update Navigation Edit `src/_data/navigation.json`: - Add, remove, or reorder nav items - Update service taglines and icons - Toggle `comingSoon` to activate/deactivate Phase 2 pages ### Add Team Members Edit `src/about.njk` — find the `.grid-4` team section and add a new: ```njk {{ teamCard("XX", "Name", "Role", "Bio text here.") }} ``` ### Update Testimonials Each testimonial uses the `testimonialCard` macro: ```njk {{ testimonialCard("Quote text", "Name", "Role / Band", "Initials") }} ``` --- ## Environment & Configuration ### `.eleventy.js` Key Settings | Setting | Value | Description | |---|---|---| | Input dir | `src` | All source files | | Output dir | `_site` | Built site (gitignored) | | Template formats | njk, html, md | Processed file types | | Passthrough | `assets`, `artist-template` | Copied as-is to output | ### Custom Filters | Filter | Usage | Description | |---|---|---| | `year` | `{{ "" \| year }}` | Current 4-digit year | ### Shortcodes | Shortcode | Usage | Description | |---|---|---| | `label` | `{% label "text" %}` | Renders a section label element | | `feature` | `{% feature "icon", "text" %}` | Renders a feature list item | ### Collections | Collection | Tag | Pages | |---|---|---| | `phase1` | `tags: phase1` | Active service pages | | `phase2` | `tags: phase2` | Coming soon pages | --- ## Browser Support The site targets modern browsers. No IE11 support. CSS custom properties, Grid, and Flexbox are used extensively. For Eleventy 2.x requirements, Node.js 14+ is required (18+ recommended). --- ## License © 2026 StageHaus Productions. All rights reserved. Site built and maintained by the StageHaus web team. Artist site templates are licensed for use by StageHaus management clients only.

  "tagline": "Built for the Stage. Driven by Results.",
  "description": "StageHaus Productions is a full-service artist production and management company offering artist management, booking strategy, show logistics, merch operations, and website development.",
  "url": "https://yourusername.github.io/stagehaus",
  "email": "hello@stagehaus.com",
  "emailArtists": "artists@stagehaus.com",
  "emailProduction": "production@stagehaus.com",
  "phone": "+1 (918) 555-0190",
  "location": "Tulsa, Oklahoma",
  "founded": "2014",
  "social": {
    "instagram": "https://instagram.com/stagehausproductions",
    "twitter": "https://twitter.com/stagehaus",
    "linkedin": "https://linkedin.com/company/stagehaus"
  },

  ]
}

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <meta name="description" content="{{ pageDescription or site.description }}" />

  <title>{% if title and title != site.name %}{{ title }} — {{ site.name }}{% else %}{{ site.name }} — {{ site.tagline }}{% endif %}</title>

  <!-- Open Graph -->
  <meta property="og:type"        content="website" />
  <meta property="og:site_name"   content="{{ site.name }}" />
  <meta property="og:title"       content="{{ title or site.name }}" />
  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ pageDescription or site.description }}" />

  <!-- Fonts -->
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin />
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Cormorant+Garamond:ital,wght@0,400;0,600;0,700;1,400;1,600&family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap" rel="stylesheet" />

  <!-- Favicon (inline SVG data URI) -->
  <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><rect width='100' height='100' rx='12' fill='%23C85D28'/><text y='.85em' font-size='62' x='14' fill='%23F0EBE1' font-family='Georgia,serif' font-weight='bold'>SH</text></svg>" />

  <!-- Styles -->
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/css/style.css" />

  <!-- Page-specific head block -->
  {% block head %}{% endblock %}
</head>
<body class="{{ bodyClass or '' }}">

  {% include "partials/header.njk" %}

  <main id="main-content">
    {{ content | safe }}
  </main>

  {% include "partials/footer.njk" %}

  <!-- Scripts -->
  <script src="/assets/js/main.js" defer></script>
  {% block scripts %}{% endblock %}

</body>
</html>

--- layout: base.njk title: About StageHaus Productions pageDescription: Learn the story behind StageHaus Productions — our founding, philosophy, team, and the values that drive everything we do for artists. currentPage: about bodyClass: page-about --- {% from "macros/components.njk" import pageHero, valueCard, teamCard, statBar %} {{ pageHero( "Our Story", "Built Backstage.
Proven on Stage.", "StageHaus Productions was founded by touring professionals who believed artists deserved better — better management, better deals, and better support at every step of the journey.", "Home", "/" ) }} {# ── ORIGIN STORY ──────────────────────────────────────────── #}
The Beginning

We Built the Company
We Always Wished Existed

In 2014, a small group of touring professionals — road managers, booking agents, and stage technicians — got tired of watching great artists get bad deals, late payments, and zero transparency from the companies supposed to be supporting them.

So they started StageHaus Productions in Tulsa, Oklahoma. Not with venture capital or a famous name attached — just a shared belief that the artist should always come first, and that operational excellence was the foundation of any sustainable career.

Today, StageHaus operates in 40+ cities across North America, has produced over 250 events, and manages a curated roster of artists across multiple genres — all while staying true to the boutique, artist-first model that started everything.

Our Founding Principle
"Every decision we make has to pass one test: is this good for the artist? If not, we don't do it."
— Devon Lake, CEO & Co-Founder
{# ── TIMELINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #}
Our Timeline

From Backstage to Boardroom

2014

StageHaus Founded

Founded in Tulsa, Oklahoma by a team of touring veterans. First year: 14 events, 3 artists, one shared phone line, and a lot of determination.

2017

Production Division Launch

Added full show logistics and production capabilities, bringing technical production in-house for the first time. This changed everything — quality control, cost efficiency, and speed all improved dramatically.

2020

Creative & Merch Expansion

Pandemic-era pivot led to building out our creative direction and merch operations divisions — helping artists generate revenue and maintain fan connection during the industry shutdown.

2024

National Expansion

Reached 40+ cities, crossed 250 total events, launched our digital services division, and began laying the groundwork for international market entry. Our biggest chapter yet is just beginning.

{# ── CORE VALUES ───────────────────────────────────────────── #}
What Drives Us

Our Core Values

These aren't poster words. They're the principles behind every deal, every tour, and every email we send.

{{ valueCard( "◈", "Artist First", "Every decision runs through one filter: what's best for the artist? Not the deal, not the margin, not the optics. The artist." ) }} {{ valueCard( "◎", "Radical Transparency", "Open-book financials, clear contracts, no hidden fees. You always know exactly where your money is and where it's going." ) }} {{ valueCard( "⬡", "Operational Excellence", "The details aren't details — they're the job. Great production is invisible because nothing goes wrong." ) }} {{ valueCard( "◆", "Boutique Focus", "We deliberately keep our roster small so every artist gets real attention. We're not scaling by adding artists — we're scaling by getting better." ) }} {{ valueCard( "◉", "Long-Game Thinking", "We optimize for 5-year careers, not 5-second headlines. Sustainable, compounding growth over quick wins." ) }} {{ valueCard( "◇", "Honest Partnership", "We push back when we think you're wrong. That's not disrespect — that's the job. The best managers tell the truth." ) }}
{# ── TEAM ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── #}
Meet the Team

The Humans Behind StageHaus

Every one of us has spent time on the road, in the venue, or in the studio. We know what artists need because we've seen it firsthand.

{{ teamCard( "DL", "Devon Lake", "CEO & Co-Founder", "20 years in live music — started as a front-of-house engineer, became a road manager, then built StageHaus to fix everything that frustrated him along the way." ) }} {{ teamCard( "MR", "Maya Reeves", "Head of Artist Management", "Former A&R exec turned artist advocate. Maya has guided artists from 500-cap rooms to arena tours, and she brings that experience to every roster decision." ) }} {{ teamCard( "JK", "Jamie Kross", "Director of Production", "Toured with 30+ acts across 15 countries. Jamie runs the tightest production operation in the mid-market — on time, on budget, no drama." ) }} {{ teamCard( "TP", "Tara Patel", "Artist Hospitality Lead", "Backstage logistics specialist with a gift for anticipating needs before artists know they have them. The person who makes tour feel like home." ) }} {{ teamCard( "CW", "Cole Weston", "Creative Director", "Brand strategist and visual designer with roots in editorial photography and music video production. Cole shapes how your audience sees you." ) }} {{ teamCard( "NB", "Nia Brooks", "Merch & Commerce Lead", "Built and scaled three artist merch brands from zero. Nia oversees product development, fulfilment, and retail strategy for the full StageHaus roster." ) }} {{ teamCard( "AS", "Aaron Sewell", "Touring & Booking Strategist", "Former agency booker who switched sides to work directly for artists. Aaron knows the touring circuit, the promoters, and how to structure deals that actually make sense." ) }} {{ teamCard( "LH", "Lena Huang", "Business Affairs", "Entertainment attorney turned in-house counsel. Lena reviews every contract, negotiates every term, and makes sure artists don't sign away what they've built." ) }}
{# ── STAT BAR ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #}

StageHaus offers capped rosters, transparent financials, and in-house support across every dimension of your career. currentPage: artist-management bodyClass: page-artist-management tags: phase1 --- {% from "macros/components.njk" import pageHero, testimonialCard %} {{ pageHero( "Artist Management", "Your Career.
Our Full Attention.", "We manage a deliberately small roster because every artist we take on gets real, consistent, hands-on support — not a quarterly check-in from someone who has 60 other clients.", "Services", "/#services" ) }} {# ── OVERVIEW ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #}
Our Approach

Management That
Actually Manages

Most management companies sign 80 artists and serve none of them well. We do the opposite: StageHaus caps its management roster and assigns a dedicated lead to every artist — one person who is accountable for your career, day in and day out.

We handle the business so you can handle the art. That means booking, deal negotiation, strategic planning, team building, brand development, and every operational detail that stands between you and the stage.

  • ✓ Dedicated manager — not a team, not an intern, a named professional
  • ✓ Strategic career roadmapping with 12/24/36-month milestones
  • ✓ Full contract negotiation — no third-party attorneys needed
  • ✓ Monthly financial reporting with full transparency
  • ✓ Team coordination — booking, PR, label, brand partners
  • ✓ Artist owns their masters — always. Non-negotiable.
The Numbers
80+
Artists Managed
12
Years Experience
40+
Cities Active
{# ── MANAGEMENT TIERS ──────────────────────────────────────── #}
Service Tiers

Management Built
to Your Stage

Every artist is at a different point in their career. Our three management tiers are designed to meet you where you are and grow with you.

Tier 1

Launchpad

For independent artists building their first real infrastructure. We establish foundations: booking, online presence, brand identity, and a realistic 12-month growth roadmap.

  • ✓Dedicated manager
  • ✓Career roadmap & milestone planning
  • ✓Booking strategy & outreach
  • ✓Monthly reporting
Apply for Launchpad
Tier 2 · Most Popular

Momentum

For artists with an established audience ready to scale. Full management plus in-house booking, production support, and brand partnership development.

  • ✓Everything in Launchpad
  • ✓Full touring & production support
  • ✓Brand partnership outreach
  • ✓Merch strategy & operations
  • ✓Sync & licensing representation
Apply for Momentum
Tier 3

Legacy

For established artists seeking comprehensive career management. Full-service operations with a dedicated account team, media relations, and long-form strategic planning.

  • ✓Everything in Momentum
  • ✓Dedicated account team (3+ specialists)
  • ✓Media & press relations
  • ✓International market strategy
  • ✓Legacy asset planning
Inquire About Legacy
{# ── DIFFERENTIATORS ───────────────────────────────────────── #}
Why StageHaus

What Makes Us Different

⊕

Capped Roster

We deliberately limit how many artists we manage at one time. This is non-negotiable. It's how we guarantee that every artist gets real attention from a real person.

⊛

In-House Everything

Booking, production, merch, web, creative — all inside StageHaus. No referrals, no margin stacking, no coordination nightmares. One team, one vision.

⊘

Artist Owns Their Masters

We will never, under any circumstances, ask for a stake in your music rights. Your art is yours. We earn our fees on services rendered — period.

⊡

Open-Book Accounting

You get a real-time financial dashboard and monthly statements showing every dollar in and out. No surprises. No hidden deductions. Full transparency, always.

--- layout: base.njk title: Booking Strategy — StageHaus Productions pageDescription: StageHaus booking strategy goes beyond filling calendar dates — we negotiate smarter deals, build routing efficiency, and position artists for sustainable touring careers. currentPage: booking-strategy bodyClass: page-booking-strategy tags: phase1 --- {% from "macros/components.njk" import pageHero, processStep, testimonialCard %} {{ pageHero( "Booking Strategy", "More Than Dates.
A Real Strategy.", "Booking is not just filling slots on a calendar. It's positioning, routing, negotiation, and relationship management — executed with precision and a long-term view of your career.", "Services", "/#services" ) }} {# ── OVERVIEW ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #}
How We Think About Booking

Every Show Should
Move the Career Forward

A bad booking strategy will have you playing too many shows for too little money in the wrong markets — burning out your team, draining your fanbase, and chasing fees instead of building leverage. We don't let that happen.

StageHaus approaches every booking cycle as a strategic campaign. We analyze your current fanbase geography, identify markets where you're punching below your weight, and build a routing plan that maximizes revenue and brand equity simultaneously.

We have long-standing relationships with promoters, venues, and festival programmers across 40+ markets — and we use those relationships to position our artists, not just find them gigs.

  • ✓ Strategic market analysis before any outreach begins
  • ✓ Routing efficiency — minimized dead miles, maximized show density
  • ✓ Deal negotiation — we know floor guarantees, backend splits, and rider norms
  • ✓ Festival submission and outreach — curated, targeted, and tracked
  • ✓ Corporate and private event opportunities when relevant
  • ✓ Support slot and co-headlining deal sourcing
Booking by the Numbers
250+
Booked Shows
40+
Active Markets
200+
Promoter Relationships
{# ── CAPABILITIES ──────────────────────────────────────────── #}
Full Scope

What Our Booking
Team Covers

◈

Club & Theater Touring

Headline and support slots at 300–3,000-cap venues. We know which rooms are right for your market level and which promoters pay on time.

◉

Festival Submissions

Targeted outreach to regional and national festivals with curated pitches. We track every submission, follow up consistently, and negotiate favorable billing.

◎

Support Slot Sourcing

We identify touring headliners with complementary audiences and negotiate strategic support deals that expand your reach into new markets.

⬡

Corporate & Private Events

Corporate gigs, private events, and brand activation shows often pay 2–5x venue rates. We identify and vet these opportunities when appropriate for your brand.

◆

Tour Routing & Logistics

We build efficient routing with geographic clustering, travel time management, and load-in coordination to minimize costs and maximize artist energy.

◇

Contract Review & Negotiation

Every offer gets reviewed before you see it. We push on guarantees, riders, marketing commitments, and settlement terms — because the first offer is rarely the final offer.

{# ── FAQ ──────────────────────────────────────────── #}
Common Questions
Do you work with artists outside of Tulsa?

Absolutely. StageHaus operates in 40+ cities across North America. Our team travels, and many of our remote services — management, booking strategy, website development, merch operations — are location-independent.

What genres do you work with?

We're genre-agnostic when it comes to production and logistics. For artist management, we work across Americana, indie rock, R&B, electronic, folk, and adjacent spaces. If your music has a live audience, we likely have relevant experience.

How do management fees work?

Management fees are a percentage of gross income, ranging by tier. We're fully transparent about our fee structure in the first call — no surprises, no hidden deductions. All financials are provided in monthly statements.

How quickly can you get a website live?

Template-based sites can go live in 3–5 business days with your content ready. Custom builds typically take 3–6 weeks depending on complexity and revision cycles. We'll give you a concrete timeline in your project brief.

Do you offer one-off production support?

Yes. While our best results come from ongoing relationships, we do provide production support for individual events — particularly larger headlining shows, festivals, and special events. Reach out with your date and venue and we'll let you know our availability.

--- layout: base.njk title: Merch Operations — StageHaus Productions pageDescription: End-to-end merch operations for independent artists. StageHaus handles product design, manufacturing, venue sales, and e-commerce fulfillment — all under one roof. currentPage: merch-operations bodyClass: page-merch-operations tags: phase1 --- {% from "macros/components.njk" import pageHero, processStep, testimonialCard %} {{ pageHero( "Merch Operations", "Revenue Beyond
the Ticket.", "Merchandise is one of the highest-margin revenue streams in an artist's business — and most artists leave most of that money on the table. We change that.", "Services", "/#services" ) }} {# ── OVERVIEW ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #}
Full-Service Merch

From Concept to Customer,
We Own the Process

Most artists deal with merch as an afterthought — a folding table at the back of the room with shirts that don't fit right. StageHaus builds merch programs that reflect the artist's brand, generate real revenue, and create lasting fan connection.

We handle the full lifecycle: concept and design in partnership with your creative vision, manufacturing through vetted suppliers, venue sales operation, and an online storefront backed by Square and Stripe — all reconciled cleanly at month-end.

  • ✓ Product concept development & design collaboration
  • ✓ Manufacturing sourcing — domestic & international suppliers
  • ✓ Venue merch table operation & trained sales staff
  • ✓ Online storefront setup, fulfillment, and customer service
  • ✓ Inventory management & reorder planning
  • ✓ Monthly revenue reporting with full margin breakdown
Why Merch Matters

"Merchandise is often an artist's highest-margin income stream — and one of the few revenue lines they actually control. We help you own it completely."

— Nia Brooks, Merch & Commerce Lead

{# ── CAPABILITIES ──────────────────────────────────────────── #}
What We Offer

The Full Merch Stack

◈

Creative & Design

Product concepts developed in collaboration with our creative team and your artistic direction. Every piece should feel like an extension of your brand — not a generic tee with a logo.

◉

Manufacturing

We work with vetted domestic and overseas suppliers for apparel, accessories, and specialty items. Quality control checks before any order ships to your warehouse or fulfillment center.

◎

Venue Sales

StageHaus-trained staff operate your merch table at every show — set up, sell, and settle out. We track every unit, handle cash and card, and give you a full end-of-night report.

⬡

Online Storefront

We set up and manage your e-commerce storefront with Square or Stripe integration, product photography, copy, and ongoing updates. Orders fulfilled from your warehouse or via print-on-demand.

◆

Inventory & Fulfillment

We manage inventory levels, reorder triggers, and shipping fulfillment — so you're never out of your best-selling shirt the week before a big tour date.

◇

Revenue Reporting

Monthly P&L reports showing venue sales, online sales, COGS, margin by SKU, and cumulative totals. You see the full picture, every time.

{# ── PROCESS ───────────────────────────────────────────────── #}
Getting Started

From Zero to Storefront
in Four Steps

{{ processStep("01", "Brand & Product Audit", "We start by reviewing your existing merch, brand assets, and fan profile to identify gaps, opportunities, and what product categories will resonate most with your audience.") }} {{ processStep("02", "Design & Sampling", "Our creative team develops initial concepts and samples. You review and approve before anything goes to production — no surprises.") }} {{ processStep("03", "Production & Storefront Launch", "Once approved, we manage manufacturing and simultaneously build your online storefront — integrated with your preferred payment processor, ready for pre-orders on day one.") }} {{ processStep("04", "Ongoing Operations", "We take it from there: venue sales, fulfillment, inventory management, and monthly reporting. You focus on making music; we keep the merch business running.") }}

Your Online Store,

--- layout: base.njk title: Show Logistics — StageHaus Productions pageDescription: Flawless show logistics powered by StageHaus Productions. Because We Do.", "Every successful live performance is built on thousands of decisions made before the artist walks on stage. We make those decisions — and we make them well.", "Services", "/#services" ) }} {# ── OVERVIEW ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #}
What We Handle

Logistics Is Where
Shows Are Won or Lost

Most artists don't realize how much work goes into a show before they arrive at the venue. Advance calls, technical riders, crew coordination, hospitality buildouts, settlement negotiations — each one is a potential point of failure if not handled by someone who knows what they're doing.

StageHaus production coordinators have overseen 250+ events across club shows, theater runs, and multi-stage festivals. We know the difference between a venue that's honest on their tech spec and one that isn't. We know what to push for in hospitality and what battles aren't worth fighting. We bring that institutional knowledge to every single show.

  • ✓ Full advance work — venue, production, hospitality, settlement
  • ✓ Day-of-show coordinator on-site at every event
  • ✓ Technical rider creation and enforcement
  • ✓ Crew coordination and local crew sourcing
  • ✓ Hospitality setup and green room management
  • ✓ Post-show settlement and financial reconciliation

{# ── SERVICE BREAKDOWN ─────────────────────────────────────── #}
Service Breakdown

Every Aspect of the Show,
Covered

◈

Production Advancing

We initiate and complete all advance calls with venue, promoter, and technical teams 4–6 weeks out. Nothing is left to the last minute.

◉

Technical Riders

We write and enforce technical riders tailored to each artist's actual needs — not a copy-paste from the last band's docs. Stage plots, input lists, backline specs, all of it.

◎

Crew & Staffing

We source, vet, and brief local crew in every market. We have relationships with trusted stagehands, backline techs, monitor engineers, and security in 40+ cities.

⬡

Hospitality & Catering

Artist rider fulfillment, green room setup, catering coordination, and guest list management. Your artists arrive to a backstage that's ready, comfortable, and stocked.

◆

Day-of-Show Management

A StageHaus coordinator is on-site from load-in through load-out — managing schedule, resolving issues, and keeping everyone on the same page so you can focus on the performance.

◇

Settlement & Reconciliation

Post-show settlement handled by our team: ticket count verification, backend splits, expense reconciliation. Clean numbers every time, no promoter games.

Our Approach

Artist Sites Designed
With Purpose

Too many artist websites are digital brochures — pretty but passive. We build sites that do work: capturing email leads, driving show ticket sales, integrating merch stores, and telling your story in a way that converts casual listeners into invested fans.

Every site we build is custom — not a Squarespace template with a logo swap. We design around your visual identity, write SEO-optimized copy, integrate your streaming links and socials, and hand you a site you're proud to put on a business card.

We also develop and maintain a library of artist site templates that StageHaus management clients can deploy in days — purpose-built for the music industry, optimized from the ground up.

  • ✓ Custom design aligned to your visual identity
  • ✓ Mobile-first, performance-optimized builds
  • ✓ Merch store integration (Square / Stripe / Bandcamp)
  • ✓ Show listings and ticketing integration
  • ✓ Email list capture and CRM integration
  • ✓ EPK (Electronic Press Kit) pages for media and booking
Tech We Build On
Eleventy (11ty)
Nunjucks Templates
GitHub Pages / Netlify
Square / Stripe
Mailchimp / ConvertKit
Figma (Design Source)
{# ── SERVICE OFFERINGS ─────────────────────────────────────── #}
What We Build

Every Type of Artist
Digital Property

◈

Artist Home Sites

Your primary digital presence — bio, music, shows, press kit, links, merch. Structured for SEO and first impressions, designed to keep visitors longer and act faster.

◉

Electronic Press Kits

Dedicated EPK pages built for industry contacts — stage/tech specs, press photos, clips, bio in multiple lengths, booking contact, and a streaming embed. Everything a booker or journalist needs.

◎

Tour & Campaign Sites

Dedicated microsites for major tours, album releases, or brand campaigns — single-focus, high-conversion pages with countdown timers, ticket links, and email capture.

⬡

Merch Storefronts

Standalone or integrated storefronts connected to Square or Stripe. Product pages, cart logic, checkout, and order confirmation — all custom-designed to match your brand.

◆

Maintenance & Hosting

We don't just build and disappear. Ongoing maintenance packages keep your site updated, fast, and secure. Show listings refreshed, merch updated, copy revised — all handled.

◇

Template Licensing

StageHaus artist site templates are available for artists who want a polished, production-tested site fast. Built on Eleventy, deployable to GitHub Pages in under an hour.

{# ── ARTIST TEMPLATE SCAFFOLD ──────────────────────────────── #}
StageHaus Templates

Ready-to-Deploy
Artist Site Templates

Our template library gives artists a head start — professionally designed, technically sound, and fully customizable. Each template is purpose-built for music industry use cases.

{# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ARTIST TEMPLATE SCAFFOLD ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── StageHaus maintains a library of reusable artist site templates located in /src/artist-template/. TEMPLATE STRUCTURE: /src/artist-template/ ├── index.html — Template preview / landing page ├── style.css — Standalone CSS for template └── README.md — Deployment and customization guide DEPLOYMENT: Templates deploy to GitHub Pages via the same GitHub Actions workflow as the main StageHaus site. Each artist gets their own repo forked from the template. CUSTOMIZATION VARIABLES (set in _data/artist.json): - artistName, artistGenre, artistBio - heroImage, logoImage, socialLinks - streamingLinks (Spotify, Apple Music, etc.) - showDates (array of upcoming events) - merchEnabled (boolean) TO ADD A NEW TEMPLATE VARIANT: 1. Duplicate /src/artist-template/ 2. Name it /src/artist-template-[style]/ (e.g. artist-template-dark) 3. Add to .eleventy.js passthrough copy list 4. Update this page with the new template card below ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #}
